examples: What he had felt earlier in the day had been a mere trifling qualm.
Did she see her husband as capable of murder? She had used the word without a qualm.
He felt a qualm in his stomach, and it was more in memory of his own loneliness than anticipation of hers.
Big city folk, you're going to have one significant qualm with the new Maps: there are no public transportation options for navigation.
The third ethical qualm involves the thorny issue of fairness.
